title: agrostis stolonifera var. varia
intro: Agrostis stolonifera var. varia, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a type of grass that is native to Europe and is commonly found in damp meadows, marshes, and along waterways. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a range of health issues including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of creeping bentgrass include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, creeping bentgrass is often used as a tea or tincture to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism.
